summary: Package overrides console.warn/console.error to exfiltrate log data to a hardcoded Telegram bot
details: On require(), index.js executes an IIFE that replaces the global console.warn and console.error with async wrappers that perform an HTTPS GET to https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat IDs (-1001161709623 for warn, -1001433099398 for error), sending the first argument as the message text. Because console is a global in Node.js, this side-effect affects every subsequent console.warn/console.error call made anywhere in the installer's application — including unrelated frameworks and dependencies — silently redirecting log content (which commonly includes stack traces, internal hostnames, user input, tokens, and error details) to an attacker-controlled Telegram chat. The destination is hardcoded and the exfiltration is triggered by normal use of the standard logging API, matching the silent-relay pattern. Separately, jq.js and t.js contain hardcoded Cloudflare credentials belonging to the author; these files are not loaded from the main entry and represent author self-harm rather than installer-directed theft, but do not affect the block decision.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
